name: Group SOP
description: The Group SOP generates groups of points or primitives according to various criteria and allows you to act upon these groups. Elements can occur in more than one group. Groups are used in many parts of the SOP Editor to specify which portion(s) of input geometry you wish a SOP to act upon.
summary: The Group SOP generates groups of points or primitives according to various criteria and allows you to act upon these groups. Elements can occur in more than one group. Groups are used in many parts o

parameters.name: Group Name
parameters.label: Group Name
parameters.description: Parameter of this operator. See operator description for details.

parameters.name: Entity
parameters.label: Entity
parameters.description:
entity -  - Primitives or Points.

 primitive - point -

parameters.name: Primitives
parameters.label: Primitives
parameters.description: primitive - point -

parameters.name: Geometry Type
parameters.label: Geometry Type
parameters.description:
geotype -  - Select the geometry type group. The selection will only pertain to the geometry type specified. e.g. If you only wanted to group polygons.

 all - All geometry will be selected. bezierc - bezier - circle - mesh - meta - nurbc - nurb - part - poly - sphere - tube - tristrip - trifan -

parameters.name: All Types
parameters.label: All Types
parameters.description: all - All geometry will be selected. bezierc - bezier - circle - mesh - meta - nurbc - nurb - part - poly - sphere - tube - tristrip - trifan -

parameters.name: Use Number
parameters.label: Use Number
parameters.description: usenumber - Allows selection of grouping of entities by number. When the Enable button is Active, the selection options become active and can be used to select entities. The fields available are listed below.

parameters.name: Create Ordered
parameters.label: Create Ordered
parameters.description: ordered - When selected, elements in the group are traversed in the order they are selected; otherwise they are traversed in creation order.

parameters.name: Operation
parameters.label: Operation
parameters.description:
groupop -  - When the Number Enable button is checked, this option groups entities based on a defined Pattern or by a Range.

 grppattern - Select a pattern in the Pattern field below. grprange - Select a Range using the Start/End and Select_of_ fields below. grpexpression - Select a range using the Filter  field below.

parameters.name: Group by Pattern
parameters.label: Group by Pattern
parameters.description: grppattern - Select a pattern in the Pattern field below. grprange - Select a Range using the Start/End and Select_of_ fields below. grpexpression - Select a range using the Filter  field below.

parameters.name: Pattern
parameters.label: Pattern
parameters.description:
pattern - Activated when Operation is set to Group by Pattern. In this field, enter the range of primitives to select. The required syntax is "S.P", where S is the index of the parent surface, and P the profile index on that surface. You can mix primitives with profiles in the list. A mixed group is automatically ordered.
For Example:

0.4 2 4 2.5 3.7 selects three profiles and two primitives,
0-100:2 selects every other number from 0 to 100,
0-10:2,3 selects every two of three,
0.0-6 selects six profiles on primitive 0,
0.* selects all profiles on primitive 0,
!4 selects every primitive or point except the fourth,
9-0 selects first ten (in reverse if ordered flag is on),
!0.* selects all profiles except those on primitive 0,
* selects all primitives or points, and no profiles.See Pattern Matching for details.

parameters.name: Transfer Selection to Pattern
parameters.label: Transfer Selection to Pattern
parameters.description:
transfer - This allows you to define the range of points / primitives visually by selecting them in the Viewport with the Select state. Clicking this button transfers the selected points/primitives into the Pattern field as a compacted range (e.g. 1-23 40 67-100). This eliminates the need for typing the point or primitive numbers manually.
Note:  and primitive selections can be dumped directly into Group fields without use of the Group . Do this by selecting the points or primitives in the Viewport with the Select state. Then the pop-up N menu beside the Group field of the  you want to cook should display the selection in the input  (e.g. "grid1's  Selection"). Ranges are automatically compacted.

parameters.name: Start / End
parameters.label: Start / End
parameters.description:
range -  - Activated when Operation is set to Group by Range. Select the start and end of the primitive/point number selection.

rangestart -rangeend -

parameters.name: Select _ of _
parameters.label: Select _ of _
parameters.description:
select -  - Activated when Operation is set to Group by Range. Select every nth occurrence of every mth entity in the above Start/End range.
For Example: entering 1 and 2 selects 1 out of every 2 entities.


select1 -select2 -

parameters.name: Filter Expression
parameters.label: Filter Expression
parameters.description: filter - The Filter  provided is evaluated for every point/primitive. Wherever it is true, the entity is added. All the local variables of point and primitive are present, though only accessable when the right type of group is being created.

parameters.name: Use Bounds
parameters.label: Use Bounds
parameters.description: usebounds - This option is used for selecting entities based on bounding volumes: Bounding Box, or Bounding Sphere. When Active, the selection options become active and can be used to select entities. The fields available are listed below. The bounding volume can be seen in the viewport as guide geometry.

parameters.name: Bounding Type
parameters.label: Bounding Type
parameters.description:
boundtype -  - Selects the type of bounding volume to use

 usebbox - Entities contained within the box are selected. usebsphere - Entities contained within the sphere are selected. usebobject - Points contained within the Object are selected. Make sure the object consists of well formed polygons. If using an irregular mesh, convert to polygons with a Convert , then use a Divide  to convex sides to 3.

parameters.name: Bounding Box
parameters.label: Bounding Box
parameters.description: usebbox - Entities contained within the box are selected. usebsphere - Entities contained within the sphere are selected. usebobject - Points contained within the Object are selected. Make sure the object consists of well formed polygons. If using an irregular mesh, convert to polygons with a Convert , then use a Divide  to convex sides to 3.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.label: Size
parameters.description:
size -  - Dimensions of either the Bounding Box or Bounding Sphere in X, Y and Z.

 sizex - sizey - sizez -

parameters.name: X
parameters.label: X
parameters.description: sizex - sizey - sizez -

parameters.name: Center
parameters.label: Center
parameters.description:
t -  - The X, Y, and Z coordinates of the center of the bounding volume.

 tx - ty - tz -

parameters.name: Use Normal
parameters.label: Use Normal
parameters.description:
usenormal - This option is used for selecting entities based on the angle of the entity normals. When the Active, the selection options become active and can be used to select entities. The fields available are listed below. 
The primary axis and the spread angle from the defined axis define a range of angles. If any entity normals lie within this range, then the associated entity is selected.
For Example: if you want to select the polygons that are very steep in a polygon mountain terrain on the XZ axis. You would set the Direction to be 0, 1, 0 and the spread angle to around 75. This selects all the polygons with normals that lie flat to fairly sloped. You will have grouped all of the polygons that lie flat up to polys that are at a 75 angle from the axis. You are left with all of the polygons that are 76 or greater.

parameters.name: Direction
parameters.label: Direction
parameters.description:
dir -  - The default values of 0, 1, 0 create a normal vector straight up in Y, which is perpendicular to the XZ plane, which becomes the primary axis. The 1, 0, 0 points the normal in positive X, giving a normal axis perpendicular to the YZ. The plane may be positioned at an angle by using values typed in (1, 1, 0 gives a 45 angle plane) or interactively by using the direction vector jack. Values between 0 and 1 should be used.

 dirx - diry - dirz -

parameters.name: Spread Angle
parameters.label: Spread Angle
parameters.description: angle - The value entered in this field generates an angle of deviation from the primary axis. This can be visualized as a cone where the radius of the base of the cone is defined by the Spread Angle and the axis of the cone is determined by the Direction axis. Viewing the primitive normals in the viewport, you can see that any primitives with normals that have an angle that lies in the range of angles defined by the cone will be selected and grouped.

parameters.name: Backface from
parameters.label: Backface from
parameters.description: camera - This menu allows you to select an object. Typically, a camera object would be chosen. The primitives which are backface when viewed from the object specified will be grouped or selected.

parameters.name: Use Edges
parameters.label: Use Edges
parameters.description: useedges - Allows you to group primitives by edges.

parameters.name: Edge Angle
parameters.label: Edge Angle
parameters.description: doangle - Enables the Edge Angle parameter.

parameters.name: Edge Depth
parameters.label: Edge Depth
parameters.description: dodepth - Enables the Edge Depth parameter.

parameters.name: Point Number
parameters.label: Point Number
parameters.description: point - Enter the specific point numbers (only for point groups).

parameters.name: Unshared Edges
parameters.label: Unshared Edges
parameters.description: unshared - When selecting points, this option selects the points of a ploygonal mesh which appear on the boundary (i.e. those which are not shared) for inclusion in the group, and orders them. In addition to polygonal meshes, this option also finds the boundaries of geo Hulls and open faces.

parameters.name: Create Boundary Groups
parameters.label: Create Boundary Groups
parameters.description:
boundarygroups - When selecting points with the Unshared Edges parameter, this option becomes available. Enabling it creates new groups of the form: __ , (two underscores) where the is the Group Name specified in the Create page. Numbers begin at zero, and increment as more groups are created.
These groups contain the points on each boundary of the surface. For example, if you have a grid with a hole in the middle of it, two new point groups are created - one containing the points for the outer boundary and one with the points from the hole. These new point groups are also ordered.

parameters.name: Group =
parameters.label: Group =
parameters.description: grpequal - Specify the group whose members you wish to edit. This can be one of the input groups or a new group created in this  specified in the Group Name parameter on the Group page.

parameters.name: Not
parameters.label: Not
parameters.description: not1 - When Off, include the members of the group specified in Group 1 parameter below. When On, include all members that are not part of the group specified in Group 1 parameter below.

parameters.name: Group 1
parameters.label: Group 1
parameters.description: grp1 - Select one of the input groups to start with, noting the setting of the Not (not1) parameter above.

parameters.name: None
parameters.label: None
parameters.description: none - No operation is performed between the groups. or - A OR B and - A AND B xor - !A OR !B sub - A minus B

parameters.name: Group 2
parameters.label: Group 2
parameters.description: grp2 - Select one of the input groups to combine with the group above, noting the setting of the Not (not2) parameter above.

parameters.name: Group 3
parameters.label: Group 3
parameters.description: grp3 - Select one of the input groups to combine with the group combination above, noting the setting of the Not (not3) parameter above.

parameters.name: Group 4
parameters.label: Group 4
parameters.description: grp4 - Select one of the input groups to combine with the group combination above, noting the setting of the Not (not4) parameter above.

parameters.name: Convert Type
parameters.label: Convert Type
parameters.description:
cnvtype -  - Converts a group from a point group to a primitive group, and vice versa.

 toprim - topoint -

parameters.name: Primitive to Point Group
parameters.label: Primitive to Point Group
parameters.description: toprim - topoint -

parameters.name: Group
parameters.label: Group
parameters.description: convertg - Name of the group to convert.

parameters.name: Convert Name
parameters.label: Convert Name
parameters.description: cnvtname - New group name.

parameters.name: Preserve Original
parameters.label: Preserve Original
parameters.description: preserve - When checked, preserves original geometry.

parameters.name: New Name
parameters.label: New Name
parameters.description: newname - Allows you to rename an existing group to something else.
